compliance_patterns.description: MENA government tenders typically require commercial registration, tax clearance, and insurance certificates
Most MENA government entities require Arabic deliverables

evaluator_insights.insight: MENA public sector evaluators generally weight technical proposals 60-70%
evaluator_insights.implication: Methodology depth and team qualifications are primary differentiators
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Invest in detailed methodology and comprehensive team profiles

evaluator_insights.insight: Local presence and regional experience are valued across GCC
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals should emphasize regional office, local team, and GCC project history
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Lead with GCC-specific case studies and highlight local team members

recommended_search_queries: GCC government consulting advisory best practices
Gulf Cooperation Council government procurement evaluation
forbidden_assumptions: Do NOT assume any specific country's regulations without RFP evidence
Do NOT reference Vision 2030 / QNV 2030 / other national visions unless the RFP mentions them
Do NOT assume specific nationalization requirements (Saudization/Qatarization/Emiratization) without RFP evidence
IMPORTANT: This is a FALLBACK pack — jurisdiction-specific compliance requires manual review
